description_long: This is a military-style attack helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a bright orange color with black markings along the tail. The helicopter features a large, four-bladed main rotor with alternating green and white stripes, and a smaller tail rotor at the end of a long, narrow tail boom. The cockpit is enclosed with multiple blue-tinted windows, providing visibility for both the pilot and co-pilot/gunner. Mounted weaponry is visible on either side of the fuselage, suggesting combat capability. The landing gear consists of two robust skids, designed for stability on various surfaces. The overall design is aggressive and functional, suitable for urban or combat scenarios.
description: A bright orange, military-style attack helicopter with striped main rotor blades, blue-tinted cockpit windows, and visible weapon mounts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the striped main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, weapon mounts, and the full length of the tail boom with its tail rotor.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the cockpit, weapon mounts, and the tail section with visible markings.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades with alternating green and white stripes, likely made of composite material, extending the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: A two-bladed tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored orange with black tips, partially protected by the tail structure.
attribute_landing_skids: Two straight, tubular landing skids made of metal, painted orange, with crossbars for stability and no visible footpads.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Three large, angular cockpit windows at the front and sides, blue-tinted with black framing, providing wide visibility for the crew.
